name: oom-kill-heap
expected_root_cause: Container exceeds memory limit and is killed by the OOM killer
expected_remediation: kubectl set resources deployment <name> --limits=memory=256Mi

name: exit-code-1-generic
expected_root_cause: Application exits with code 1 indicating unhandled runtime error
expected_remediation: kubectl logs scenario-02-exit-code-1 --previous to inspect error

name: exit-code-137-oom
expected_root_cause: Process killed by signal 9 (SIGKILL) due to OOM condition, exit code 137
expected_remediation: kubectl set resources deployment <name> --limits=memory=512Mi

name: exit-code-139-segfault
expected_root_cause: Segmentation fault (exit code 139) caused by invalid memory access
expected_remediation: kubectl describe pod scenario-04-segfault to check resource limits and image integrity

name: ephemeral-storage-full
expected_root_cause: Container evicted because ephemeral storage usage exceeded the configured limit
expected_remediation: kubectl set resources pod <name> --limits=ephemeral-storage=2Gi

name: liveness-probe-fail
expected_root_cause: Liveness probe repeatedly fails causing kubelet to restart the container
expected_remediation: kubectl edit pod scenario-06-liveness to fix probe path or increase failureThreshold

name: readiness-never-ready
expected_root_cause: Readiness probe never succeeds so pod stays in NotReady state indefinitely
expected_remediation: kubectl edit pod scenario-07-readiness to correct probe endpoint or remove incorrect probe

name: init-container-fail
expected_root_cause: Init container exits with error preventing the main container from starting
expected_remediation: kubectl logs scenario-08-init-fail -c init-checker --previous to debug init container

name: cpu-limit-throttle-crash
expected_root_cause: Container process starved of CPU due to a very low CPU limit causing timeout and crash
expected_remediation: kubectl set resources deployment <name> --limits=cpu=500m

name: disk-write-permission-denied
expected_root_cause: Container cannot write to mounted path due to read-only root filesystem
expected_remediation: kubectl edit pod scenario-10-readonly to remove readOnlyRootFilesystem or add writable emptyDir volume

name: too-many-open-files
expected_root_cause: Process hits file descriptor ulimit causing EMFILE errors and crash
expected_remediation: kubectl edit pod scenario-11-fd-limit to increase nofile limit in securityContext

name: job-backoff-limit
expected_root_cause: Kubernetes Job exceeds backoffLimit due to repeated container failure
expected_remediation: kubectl describe job scenario-12-job to inspect failure reason then fix container command

name: oom-kill-java-heap
expected_root_cause: JVM heap exhausted because container memory limit is lower than JVM heap size
expected_remediation: kubectl set resources deployment <name> --limits=memory=1Gi or set -Xmx to below container limit

name: pod-evicted-node-pressure
expected_root_cause: Pod evicted by kubelet due to node memory pressure condition
expected_remediation: kubectl describe node to check memory pressure then reschedule pod to node with sufficient resources

name: startup-probe-timeout
expected_root_cause: Startup probe times out before application initialises killing the container
expected_remediation: kubectl edit pod scenario-15-startup to increase startupProbe failureThreshold or initialDelaySeconds

name: container-cannot-run-as-root
expected_root_cause: Container requires root but pod security policy forbids running as root user
expected_remediation: kubectl edit pod scenario-16-noroot to add runAsNonRoot false or fix image to not require root

name: crash-loop-exit-2
expected_root_cause: Container exits with code 2 indicating misuse of shell built-in or command not found
expected_remediation: kubectl logs scenario-17-exit-2 --previous to identify missing binary or incorrect shell syntax

name: missing-env-var
expected_root_cause: Application crashes because required environment variable DATABASE_URL is not set
expected_remediation: kubectl set env deployment <name> DATABASE_URL=<value>

name: wrong-image-tag
expected_root_cause: Pod fails to start because the specified image tag does not exist in the registry
expected_remediation: kubectl set image pod/scenario-19-bad-tag crasher=busybox:latest

name: missing-secret-ref
expected_root_cause: Pod cannot start because it references a Secret that does not exist in the namespace
expected_remediation: kubectl create secret generic app-secret --from-literal=API_KEY=<value>

name: missing-configmap-ref
expected_root_cause: Pod cannot start because it references a ConfigMap that does not exist
expected_remediation: kubectl create configmap app-config --from-literal=LOG_LEVEL=info

name: bad-command-syntax
expected_root_cause: Container entrypoint command has incorrect syntax causing immediate exit
expected_remediation: kubectl edit pod scenario-22-bad-cmd to fix command array syntax

name: wrong-port-in-service
expected_root_cause: Service selector matches pod but targetPort does not match container port causing connection refused
expected_remediation: kubectl edit service scenario-23-svc to correct targetPort to match container port

name: invalid-env-value-type
expected_root_cause: Application expects integer environment variable but received string causing parse failure
expected_remediation: kubectl set env deployment <name> MAX_CONNECTIONS=100

name: volume-mount-path-conflict
expected_root_cause: Two volume mounts share the same mountPath causing filesystem conflict and crash
expected_remediation: kubectl edit pod scenario-25-mount-conflict to assign unique mountPaths to each volume

name: wrong-working-dir
expected_root_cause: Container workingDir does not exist in the image causing startup failure
expected_remediation: kubectl edit pod scenario-26-workdir to set workingDir to a path that exists in the image

name: cronjob-bad-schedule
expected_root_cause: CronJob fails to schedule because the cron expression is syntactically invalid
expected_remediation: kubectl edit cronjob scenario-27-cronjob to fix schedule to a valid cron expression

name: image-entrypoint-not-found
expected_root_cause: Container entrypoint binary does not exist in the specified image
expected_remediation: kubectl edit pod scenario-28-no-entrypoint to correct command to a binary that exists in the image

name: deployment-wrong-selector
expected_root_cause: Deployment selector does not match pod template labels so no pods are managed
expected_remediation: kubectl edit deployment scenario-29-bad-selector to align selector.matchLabels with template.metadata.labels

name: config-file-missing-in-image
expected_root_cause: Application expects a config file at a hardcoded path that does not exist in the image
expected_remediation: kubectl edit pod scenario-30-missing-cfg to mount a ConfigMap providing the missing config file

name: replica-count-zero
expected_root_cause: Deployment has replicas set to 0 so no pods are scheduled
expected_remediation: kubectl scale deployment scenario-31-zero-replicas --replicas=1

name: env-var-empty-string
expected_root_cause: Required environment variable is set but empty causing application validation failure
expected_remediation: kubectl set env deployment <name> REQUIRED_TOKEN=<non-empty-value>

name: wrong-image-repo
expected_root_cause: Image pull fails because the repository name is misspelled and does not exist
expected_remediation: kubectl set image pod/scenario-33-bad-repo crasher=busybox:latest

name: resource-request-exceeds-node
expected_root_cause: Pod cannot be scheduled because resource request exceeds available capacity on any node
expected_remediation: kubectl edit pod scenario-34-unschedulable to reduce resource requests to fit available node capacity

name: imagepullbackoff-private-registry
expected_root_cause: Image pull fails because registry requires authentication and no imagePullSecret is configured
expected_remediation: kubectl create secret docker-registry regcred --docker-server=<registry> --docker-username=<user> --docker-password=<pass> then add imagePullSecrets to pod spec

name: service-account-missing
expected_root_cause: Pod references a ServiceAccount that does not exist in the namespace
expected_remediation: kubectl create serviceaccount app-sa

name: rbac-forbidden-api-call
expected_root_cause: Application receives HTTP 403 Forbidden from Kubernetes API because ServiceAccount lacks required RBAC permissions
expected_remediation: kubectl create clusterrolebinding app-binding --clusterrole=view --serviceaccount=default:default

name: dns-resolution-failure
expected_root_cause: Application cannot resolve internal service DNS name indicating CoreDNS issue or incorrect service name
expected_remediation: kubectl get svc to verify service exists then check CoreDNS with kubectl logs -n kube-system -l k8s-app=kube-dns

name: network-policy-blocks-egress
expected_root_cause: NetworkPolicy blocks egress traffic causing application to fail when connecting to external service
expected_remediation: kubectl edit networkpolicy to add egress rule allowing traffic to required destination

name: clusterip-no-endpoints
expected_root_cause: Service has no endpoints because no pods match its selector causing connection refused
expected_remediation: kubectl get endpoints scenario-40-svc to confirm empty endpoints then fix service selector to match running pods

name: imagepullbackoff-rate-limit
expected_root_cause: Docker Hub pull rate limit exceeded causing ImagePullBackOff
expected_remediation: kubectl create secret docker-registry dockerhub-creds to authenticate and bypass rate limit

name: tls-cert-verify-fail
expected_root_cause: Application TLS handshake fails because server certificate is self-signed or expired
expected_remediation: kubectl create secret tls app-tls --cert=cert.pem --key=key.pem and mount it into the pod

name: service-port-mismatch
expected_root_cause: Service port does not match the port the container is actually listening on
expected_remediation: kubectl edit service scenario-43-svc to set targetPort to match the container listening port

name: pod-to-pod-network-unreachable
expected_root_cause: Pod cannot reach another pod due to CNI plugin misconfiguration
expected_remediation: kubectl describe node to check CNI plugin status and restart CNI daemonset if needed

name: ingress-backend-not-found
expected_root_cause: Ingress resource references a backend Service that does not exist
expected_remediation: kubectl get svc to verify backend service exists and create it if missing

name: rbac-missing-clusterrole
expected_root_cause: ClusterRoleBinding references a ClusterRole that does not exist causing authorization failure
expected_remediation: kubectl create clusterrole app-role --verb=get,list,watch --resource=pods

name: external-service-timeout
expected_root_cause: Application times out connecting to external database because no egress is allowed from the cluster
expected_remediation: kubectl edit networkpolicy to add egress rule for the external database IP and port

name: imagepullbackoff-wrong-pullsecret
expected_root_cause: ImagePullBackOff because imagePullSecret exists but contains wrong credentials
expected_remediation: kubectl delete secret regcred && kubectl create secret docker-registry regcred with correct credentials

name: coredns-pod-not-found
expected_root_cause: CoreDNS pods are not running causing all in-cluster DNS resolution to fail
expected_remediation: kubectl rollout restart deployment coredns -n kube-system

name: node-not-ready-pod-pending
expected_root_cause: Pod stuck in Pending state because all nodes are in NotReady status
expected_remediation: kubectl describe node to find taint or condition then kubectl drain and uncordon to restore scheduling
